title: monticalia pulchella subsp. guantivana
intro: Monticalia pulchella subsp. guantivana, also known as Guantiva, is a plant species within the daisy family, native to the Andean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can be used to treat wounds and reduce fever. In traditional medicine, Guantiva is used to promote overall health and well-being, with potential benefits including reduced stress, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. The plant is typically prepared as an infusion or tincture, often combined with other herbs to create a medicinal tea or syrup.
